Dr. Victor Odumuyiwa Associate Professor of Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence), Director, NITDA IT Hub (NITHUB), University of Lagos National AI and Digital Strategy in Nigeria and Teacher Professional Development "Technology is just a tool. In terms of getting the kids working together and motivating them, the teacher is the most important." — Bill Gates National Artificial Intelligence Strategy This comprehensive National AI Strategy outlines five key pillars that will propel Nigeria towards achieving its vision. Each pillar is supported by strategic objectives and practical initiatives that drive progress across the AI development and adoption spectrum. Source: NAIS (2025) Nigerian AI Ecosystem Mapping Al Learning/Practice Communities Hyperscalers/Data Innovation Government-led Al Global Al Research Training/ Centers for Al-as-a- Sandbox/GPU providers Research-NCAIR, Nigeria Professional/Community Gender-specific centres with Nigerian learners network/ Artificial Intelligence of Practice groups Allearning groups for local innovators service networks associations Research Scheme Local/Independent Global Research funders AlProfessional Al Services and Al Skils Research Centres Players with local projects Services/Consulting Strategy groups certification partners groups AlIncubators/ Al Investors Al Research Accelerators Research Application Centres of Groups in other Excellence at local Universities domains (eg, Health) Domain-specific General Al start-ups Alstart-ups Development agencies/ AlData Al Enablement and Philanthropies Collection Ecosystem funding Alfor Organisations support Organizations grand innovations Al Model Al expert domain Government Regulatory and Non-profits/ Al Solution/Developer development GPL/ application Advocacy Agencies Innovation Hubs for Government funded eg. NITDA, NDRC, LASRIC Civil society groups Support Systems Platform access champions Al ideation and support Al Hub access (eg. Responsible All providers (Source: Compiled by NAIS Workshop facilitators) Nigeria Curriculum Reform (2025) The federal Ministry of Education released a press release on the 3rd of September, overhauling its national curriculum to create a more skill-driven, future-ready education system aligned with global standards. The reform reduces subject overload and focuses on deeper learning and practical skills, including vocational and technology-related competencies. Introduction of trade and technical skills (e.g., digital and technical fields) aims to prepare students for the digital economy and workforce readiness. Curriculum restructuring supports a shift toward competency-based learning, aligning with national goals for AI and digital transformation. Source: education.gov.ng The Federal Ministry of Education Digital Learning Policy The policy positions digital learning as a national transformation tool, not just an ICT add-on. It aims to create a coherent national digital education ecosystem (not fragmented digital initiatives). The policy can be summarized into these three keywords (Federal Ministry of Education, 2023): Source: education.gov.ng, 2023 The policy aims to: Transform Nigeria’s education system through digital technology for inclusive, high- quality learning at scale. Expand equitable access to learning for underserved, remote, and vulnerable populations. Improve learning outcomes through data-driven, engaging, and skills-focused education. Build scalable, reliable, and interoperable digital education infrastructure. Strengthen teacher capacity in digital literacy and modern pedagogy. Develop and deploy high-quality, curriculum-aligned digital learning content. Ensure strong governance, data protection, and online safety frameworks. Mobilize sustainable funding through public-private partnerships and stakeholders. Establish robust monitoring and evaluation systems to track impact and progress. Implement a coordinated national rollout across federal,

capacity in digital literacy and modern pedagogy. Develop and deploy high-quality, curriculum-aligned digital learning content. Ensure strong governance, data protection, and online safety frameworks. Mobilize sustainable funding through public-private partnerships and stakeholders. Establish robust monitoring and evaluation systems to track impact and progress. Implement a coordinated national rollout across federal, state, and local levels. Source: education.gov.ng, 2023 The Digital Learning Policy (2023) will be given life from several existing policy and strategy documents that are not limited to the following: Source: education.gov.ng, 2023 Role of Artificial Intelligence in Education (Policy Insight) Source: education.gov.ng Teacher Professional Development in Nigeria Continuous Professional Development (CPD) is made mandatory for teachers to maintain their professional license. Teachers participate in accredited training programs provided by approved institutions and organizations. TRAINING FORMATS INCLUDE: Source: TRCN Teachers Registration Council of Nigeria (TRCN) Digital Platforms TRCN Digital Portal (LMS) / Teacher Portal: This portal acts as a central hub for teacher data and development tracking. Teachers are supported in continuous professional development (CPD) and professional exams. Website Continuous Professional Development (CPD) accredited platforms: This is the official licensed provider for online courses, workshops & training programs, and offers blended learning (online + in-person). Website TeachNG (TRCN-approved): They provide mobile-friendly courses and AI- driven personalized learning. Website Source: TRCN How can AI be used positively for Teaching and Learning? TWO KEY PATHWAYS Flipped Classrooms in an AI Era The Flipped Classroom method fosters effective learning, builds critical thinking, and aligns with Bloom's Taxonomy in education. The Six Levels of Bloom's Taxonomy are: Designing Evaluation in an AI-Enabled Classroom Project-Based Reflective Prompt Assessments (Oral Explanations defence) Documentation Students explain Emphasize originality, Students explain and how answers were creativity and defend their work derived real-world context AI-enabled classrooms are not just about teaching tools, it is about cultivating mindsets, building strong foundations in critical thinking, and encouraging adaptability and continuous learning among students. Thank You!
